findings.evidence: Field `unipotent_radical_trivial : True` at line 64; True is trivially provable, so ReductiveGroup k A ↔ AffineAlgGroup k A.
findings.fix: Remove the True field. Either define the unipotent radical and its triviality after base-change to the algebraic closure first, or leave ReductiveGroup as an explicit TODO/abbreviation of AffineAlgGroup.
findings.issue: ReductiveGroup.unipotent_radical_trivial : True makes the class equivalent to AffineAlgGroup — the unipotent-radical condition is a vacuous placeholder.

findings.evidence: Field `radical_trivial : True` at line 69; True is trivially provable, so SemisimpleGroup k A ↔ ReductiveGroup k A.
findings.fix: Same as above: define the radical first or mark as TODO.
findings.issue: SemisimpleGroup.radical_trivial : True makes the class equivalent to ReductiveGroup — the radical condition is a vacuous placeholder.

findings.evidence: AlgPoints defined at line 39; GroupLike defined in Mathlib as {a : A // IsGroupLikeElem R a} (grep Mathlib/RingTheory/Coalgebra/GroupLike.lean:66); for the coordinate ring of 𝔾ₘ = k[t,t⁻¹], GroupLike ≅ ℤ while G(k) = kˣ — distinct groups.
findings.fix: Define IsUnipotent on AlgPoints k A, or correct the docstring to describe what is actually formalized (a predicate on group-like elements / characters).
findings.issue: IsUnipotent (g : GroupLike k A) contradicts the docstring which says it is about g ∈ G(k). For the coordinate-ring approach, G(k) = AlgPoints k A (algebra homomorphisms A → k, line 39), not GroupLike k A (group-like elements of A, i.e. characters). The AlgPoints definition exists but is unused.

summary: ReductiveGroup and SemisimpleGroup are vacuous True placeholders, not real definitions. IsUnipotent is defined on GroupLike k A (characters) but advertised as a predicate on G(k) (algebra homomorphisms); these are mathematically distinct in the coordinate-ring approach. All three are semantic blocks.
